A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of the cash a company may generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to today to give a single present value per share.

When all those future cash flows are discounted to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of DKK 745.09 per share. Compared with the current share price of around DKK 439.80, this implies the stock is 41.0% undervalued based on this DCF framework.

data.title: Tetra Tech’s US$99 Million Navy Win And What It Could Mean
data.description:
• Tetra Tech (NasdaqGS:TTEK) has been awarded a single award, multi year US$99 million contract by NAVFAC Southeast.
  • The contract covers engineering and design services for U.S. Navy facilities, in partnership with Mason & Hanger.
  • The award positions the company for continued work on complex defense and infrastructure projects over the contract term.

Tetra Tech focuses on consulting, engineering, program management, construction management and technical services, with a presence in water, environmental and infrastructure work. Defense related infrastructure has been an area of interest for many contractors as militaries invest in resilient facilities and environmental compliance. For readers tracking NasdaqGS:TTEK, this kind of contract can be part of long duration project pipelines.

Investors may watch how Tetra Tech executes on this Navy work and whether it leads to follow on opportunities within NAVFAC or other defense agencies. The size and multi year nature of the award also provide another data point when weighing the balance between Tetra Tech’s government and commercial exposure over time.

This US$99 million, 5 year, single award contract looks like a meaningful win for Tetra Tech’s defense focused consulting and engineering business. A sole source task order gives the joint venture tight control of scope and execution, rather than sharing volume with multiple vendors. Partnering with Mason & Hanger also helps Tetra Tech cover a broad set of architect engineer capabilities for waterfront, marine and other shore based facilities, which can matter when competing against peers such as AECOM, Jacobs Solutions and WSP Global for future work.

  • Concentration in U.S. federal contracts was flagged as a risk in the narrative, so another large defense related award could reinforce that dependence if commercial and international projects do not keep pace.
  • The joint venture structure and contract scope for waterfront and non military projects may not be fully reflected in earlier commentary that focused more on disaster response and prior agency mix. Investors might therefore treat this as an additional data point on how the public sector pipeline is evolving.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Executing a complex, multi year architect engineer scope through a joint venture adds coordination and delivery risk if cost, schedule or performance issues arise.
  • 🎁 The 5 year visibility from this single award contract can support planning for staffing, technology investment and capacity allocation in Tetra Tech’s federal focused segment.
  • 🎁 Successful performance could strengthen references with NAVFAC and related agencies, which may help when competing with other engineers for future military and civil infrastructure work.

From here, you may want to keep an eye on how quickly task orders ramp under this contract, how the work mix splits between military and non military projects, and whether Tetra Tech discloses margin characteristics for this program relative to its average. data.description:

  • The new offering integrates RingCentral’s RingCX and AI Conversation Expert tools, bringing omnichannel capabilities and advanced analytics across voice, video and digital channels.
  • The launch targets mid market and enterprise clients that use Spectrum Business services and want more data driven insight from their customer interactions.

For you as an investor, this move sits at the intersection of telecom infrastructure and cloud based communications software. Charter is using its Spectrum Business footprint to offer more than connectivity by adding AI powered tools that can plug into existing workflows and CRM systems. This reflects a broader theme across business communications in which buyers look for a single provider to handle managed networks and front line customer engagement tools.

The expanded RingCentral partnership may also affect how Charter positions Spectrum Business relative to other cable and telecom peers that sell into mid market and enterprise accounts. A key factor will be how effectively Charter can translate this AI centric suite into deeper customer relationships and wider adoption across its existing business client base.

Because the platform runs over Charter’s managed network, the company is tying its core infrastructure to software that touches sales execution and customer service, which can increase switching costs for mid-market and enterprise clients if it gains traction.

From here, it is worth watching how quickly Charter can sign UCX deployments with Spectrum Business customers and whether those contracts expand over time to include more seats and features. data.description:

data.title: CareCloud (CCLD) Profitability Turn Challenges Dilution Focus After FY 2025 Earnings
data.description:
CareCloud (CCLD) has capped FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$34.4 million and basic EPS of US$0.04, alongside trailing 12 month revenue of US$120.5 million and basic EPS of US$0.10, marking a clean move away from the losses seen earlier in the period. The company has seen quarterly revenue step up from US$27.4 million in Q2 2025 to US$34.4 million in Q4 2025, while quarterly basic EPS has shifted from a loss of US$0.04 in Q1 2025 to positive readings in each subsequent quarter, pointing to firmer profitability.   • Bulls argue that this move into the black and the expected 47.7% annual earnings growth point to a business with improving economics. However, the step up from quarterly net income of US$0.01 million in Q4 2024 to US$1.5 million in Q4 2025 also means any slowdown in that pace would directly test how realistic those bullish growth assumptions really are.
    • Supporters highlight that trailing revenue rose from US$110.8 million to US$120.5 million while margins improved enough to swing to a US$3.9 million profit, which lines up with the idea of better earnings quality.

Losses Narrow on a Trailing 12‑Month View

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Century Therapeutics reported US$109.2 million of revenue with a net loss of US$9.6 million, compared with a net loss of US$126.6 million on US$6.6 million of revenue in the prior half year included in that period.
  • For a more optimistic, bullish style view, what stands out is that losses have been reduced at about 6.8% per year over the past five years. However, the latest trailing twelve month figures still show a net loss of US$9.6 million and basic EPS of US$0.11 in the red, which keeps the focus on how durable that loss reduction really is.

EPS Swings Between Halves Remain Wide

  • Across the last three reported half year periods, basic EPS moved from a loss of US$0.82 in early 2024, to a loss of US$0.79 in the second half of 2024, then to a profit of US$0.51 in the first half of 2025, highlighting a wide range of outcomes across a short timeline.
  • Bears argue that earnings are still fragile, and that view is supported by forecasts calling for earnings to decline by about 20.7% per year over the next three years, even after the swing to positive EPS in the latest half. This underlines how one profitable half does not yet offset a recent pattern of losses and the current trailing twelve month loss position.

P/S of 4.1x Sits Below Biotech Peers

  • The current P/S ratio of 4.1x sits well below the 14x level for peers and the 12.2x average for the US Biotech industry, even though analysts expect Century Therapeutics to remain unprofitable over the next three years and have also forecast no revenue next year.
  • For a more cautious, bearish narrative, what is striking is that the lower P/S multiple comes alongside substantial shareholder dilution over the past year and higher share price volatility in the last three months. Together, these factors suggest the market is weighing that valuation discount against a backdrop of continued forecast losses and no near term profitability in sight.
